title: Evax pygmaea
taxonomy: Evax pygmaea is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae. It is native to the Iberian Peninsula and southwestern France. The plant belongs to the genus Evax, which comprises a small group of annual or biennial plants. Evax pygmaea is often found in dry areas and sandy soils. The plant is also known as dwarf cotton thistle or dwarf cottonweed.
uses: Evax pygmaea, also known as Dwarf Evax, is a plant species that has been used in traditional medicine, particularly in the Mediterranean region. Although limited information is available, it has been reported to have anti-inflammatory properties and has been used to treat various ailments, including digestive issues and skin conditions. intro: Evax pygmaea, also known as dwarf evax or dwarf false mayweed, is a small, annual herbaceous plant belonging to the daisy family (Asteraceae). This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, with potential health benefits including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which may aid in the treatment of wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Evax pygmaea include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Evax pygmaea is commonly used in herbal preparations, such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, as well as in topical creams and ointments for skin and wound care.
